steps.title: Recognize the opening signal
steps.content: The text opens a parenthetical element with an em dash after “pantheon—including.” This signals that the list of three deities is an appositive phrase that must be closed before the main clause continues.

steps.title: Apply the closing rule
steps.content:
Parenthetical phrases opened with an em dash must close with a matching em dash.

The closing dash goes after “Crius” to close the appositive phrase.

A semicolon would incorrectly suggest another list item, a comma would fail to close the parenthetical, and no punctuation would leave it open.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
